summary.verdict_desc: 本周指标矩阵相对上周保持稳定（5 红 / 7 黄 / 11 绿），整体红灯比例 21.7% 仍处观察区间下沿。但估值 2/3 红（CAPE 42.05、Top-5 28.5%）叠加资金面 3/4 红（Mag4 FCF -91%、VC AI 80%、NVDA 客户投资/收入 67%）持续触发分类强制升级——历史上估值+资金面双类共振是泡沫顶部最一致的领先信号。本周新增信号：Anthropic ARR 升至 $30B 超越 OpenAI、Cerebras 5 月登陆纳斯达克、Powell 5/15 离任由 Warsh 接棒；估值与资金面双重共振触发分类强制升级。
summary.verdict_desc_en: Indicator matrix remained stable week-over-week (5 red / 7 yellow / 11 green); aggregate red ratio 21.7% sits at the low end of the Observation band. Yet valuation 2/3 RED (CAPE 42.05, Top-5 28.5%) combined with capital 3/4 RED (Mag4 FCF -91%, VC AI 80%, NVDA customer-investment-to-revenue 67%) again triggers the category-override rule — historically the most consistent leading combination at bubble tops. New developments this week: Anthropic ARR reached $30B, surpassing OpenAI; Cerebras debuts on Nasdaq this month; Powell exits May 15 with Warsh stepping in. Valuation and capital flows dual-class resonance triggers forced classification upgrade.

wow_changes.note: 等权重 vs 市值加权的 6 月差由 -3.4% 收窄至 -1.5%，RSP 跑赢幅度收敛，头部集中度有再启迹象
wow_changes.note_en: Equal-weight vs cap-weighted 6M spread narrowed from -3.4% to -1.5% as RSP's lead compressed — early sign of top-heavy regime restarting

wow_changes.note: Cerebras S-1 提交后 5 月登陆纳斯达克（$26.6B 估值，募资 $3.5B），AI IPO 管道首单从公告变成现实
wow_changes.note_en: After filing S-1, Cerebras lists on Nasdaq in May ($26.6B valuation, $3.5B raise) — the AI IPO pipeline's first major name transitions from announcement to reality

wow_changes.note: Powell 5/15 卸任主席并继续留任理事；Warsh 提名通过参议院银行委员会，6/16-17 FOMC 将由其主持，市场仍预期 2026 年仅 1 次降息
wow_changes.note_en: Powell steps down as Chair on May 15 (remains as Governor); Warsh's nomination cleared the Senate Banking Committee, set to chair the June 16-17 FOMC; the dot plot still implies just one cut in 2026

wow_changes.note: Anthropic ARR 单月再增 $11B 至 $30B，正式反超 OpenAI 的 $24B / $2B 月跑步速；二阶导持续为正
wow_changes.note_en: Anthropic ARR jumped another $11B to $30B in a single month, formally overtaking OpenAI's $24B ($2B/month pace); 2nd derivative remains decisively positive

wow_changes.note: CloudHQ $1.4B 5 年期 AAA 4 月以 125bp 定价，较 Switch 3 月 18 日的 185bp 收窄 60bps；ABS 市场对 AI 数据中心抵押品仍报以紧利差
wow_changes.note_en: CloudHQ's $1.4B 5Y AAA tranche priced at 125bp over I-curve in April, 60bp tighter than Switch's 185bp print on March 18 — the ABS market continues to bid AI data center collateral with tight spreads

indicators.note: Multpl 持平 42.05（YCharts 月度读数 39.58），155 年来第 2 高，距 2000 年峰值 44.19 仅 5%；隐含未来 10 年年化回报约 1.3%，较 20 年均值 27.5 高 48.6%
indicators.note_en: Multpl holds at 42.05 (YCharts monthly print 39.58), the 2nd highest in 155 years and just 5% below the 2000 peak of 44.19; implied 10-year forward return ~1.3%, 48.6% above the 20-year average of 27.5

indicators.note: NVDA ~7.0 + AAPL ~6.3 + MSFT ~4.6 + GOOGL ~5.5 + AMZN ~5.1 ≈ 28.5%；Top10 仍超 36%；仍远高于 2000 年峰值 ~18%
indicators.note_en: NVDA ~7.0 + AAPL ~6.3 + MSFT ~4.6 + GOOGL ~5.5 + AMZN ~5.1 ≈ 28.5%; Top 10 still above 36%; well above the 2000 peak of ~18%

indicators.note: GuruFocus 26.0、StockAnalysis 25.8、Yahoo 24.5；分析师预计 FY27 EPS $8.34，对应 fwd P/E 仅 23.8；估值反而是 Mag7 中最低
indicators.note_en: GuruFocus 26.0, StockAnalysis 25.8, Yahoo 24.5; analysts model FY27 EPS of $8.34, implying a forward P/E of just 23.8; lowest forward multiple among Mag7

indicators.threshold_text: 指引下调=红 / 加速=黄 / 稳健=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Guidance cut = red / accelerating = yellow / steady = green
indicators.note: Q1 财报后全员上调：MSFT $190B、AMZN $200B、GOOGL $180-190B（由 $175-185B 上调）、META $125-145B（由 $115-135B 上调），合计 ~$700-725B，YoY +75-77%；指引仍在加速
indicators.note_en: Across-the-board Q1 raises: MSFT $190B, AMZN $200B, GOOGL $180-190B (from $175-185B), META $125-145B (from $115-135B); aggregate ~$700-725B, YoY +75-77%; guidance still accelerating

indicators.note: Amazon Q1 FCF 同比 -95% 至 $1.2B（capex 单季 $44.2B 几乎吃光 OCF），Bernstein/Pivotal 测算 Mag4 全年 FCF 可能转负；Meta、MSFT 同步承压
indicators.note_en: Amazon Q1 FCF -95% YoY at just $1.2B (single-quarter capex of $44.2B nearly consumed all OCF); Bernstein/Pivotal model Mag4 full-year FCF possibly turning negative; Meta and MSFT under similar pressure

indicators.name_zh: AI 占 VC 投资比重
indicators.note: Crunchbase Q1 2026 全球 VC 80% 流向 AI；2 月单月更达 90%（OpenAI $40B + Anthropic $13B + xAI 等推动）；PitchBook 截至 Q3 测算累计 65%
indicators.note_en: Crunchbase Q1 2026: 80% of global VC dollars flowed to AI; February alone hit 90% (driven by OpenAI $40B, Anthropic $13B, xAI etc.); PitchBook YTD-through-Q3 estimate runs ~65%

indicators.note: NVDA 2026 年至今股权投资突破 $40B（OpenAI $30B + Anthropic 至 $10B + xAI + CoreWeave $2B + Nebius $2B + IREN $2.1B + Corning $3.2B），叠加客户承诺 ~$110B；Huang 称 OpenAI 为 "最后一笔"，但 circular financing 担忧加剧
indicators.note_en: NVDA's 2026 YTD equity bets cross $40B (OpenAI $30B + Anthropic up to $10B + xAI + CoreWeave $2B + Nebius $2B + IREN $2.1B + Corning $3.2B), plus customer commitments ~$110B; Huang called OpenAI 'the last one,' yet circular-financing concerns deepen

indicators.note: S5FI 维持 51.19%（区间 50.09-52.48），略高于 50 中线但动量明显不及上月 71%；仍处黄区
indicators.note_en: S5FI holds at 51.19% (range 50.09-52.48), only marginally above the 50 midpoint and well off last month's 71%; remains in yellow

indicators.name_zh: 市值加权 vs 等权重
indicators.note: SPY YTD +4.98% vs RSP YTD +6.33%，等权重领先收窄至 1.35pp；6M 差由 -3.4% 收敛到约 -1.5%，头部独涨阶段尚未重启但缓冲在变薄
indicators.note_en: SPY YTD +4.98% vs RSP YTD +6.33%; equal-weight lead compressed to 1.35pp; the 6M spread narrowed from -3.4% to about -1.5% — top-heavy regime has not restarted yet but the cushion is thinning

indicators.name_zh: AI 龙头内部人卖买比
indicators.note: NVDA 3/20 25 笔卖出合计 $45.8M、AVGO 4 月仅为股票奖励和税款支付（非真实卖出）；30 日窗口数据获取受限，沿用上周综合卖买比 ~8x，距 2000 极值 23x 仍远
indicators.note_en: NVDA logged 25 sale transactions totaling $45.8M on 3/20; AVGO April activity was awards and tax-related only (not real sales); 30-day window data is gated, so we carry over last week's aggregate sell/buy ratio of ~8x, still far from the 2000 peak of 23x

indicators.threshold_text: 洪流=红 / 升温=黄 / 平静=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Flood = red / Warming = yellow / Quiet = green
indicators.note: Cerebras 5 月登陆纳斯达克（CBRS，$26.6B 估值、募资 $3.5B）成为管道首单；SpaceX 6 月路演（$1.75T 估值、募资 $75B+）、OpenAI Q4 IPO（$1T 估值）、Anthropic 10 月（$380B）；合计 mega-IPO 需求 $100-200B 超 2025 全年美国 IPO
indicators.note_en: Cerebras debuts on Nasdaq in May (CBRS, $26.6B valuation, $3.5B raise) — first pipeline name to actually list; SpaceX June roadshow ($1.75T valuation, $75B+ raise), OpenAI Q4 IPO (~$1T), Anthropic October ($380B); combined mega-IPO demand $100-200B exceeds the entire 2025 US IPO market

indicators.note: ICE BofA HY OAS 维持 2.79%、12 月百分位仅 27，年初 ~4% 至今压缩 ~120bps；信用市场对 AI capex 与广度走弱仍未紧缩
indicators.note_en: ICE BofA HY OAS holds at 2.79%, 12-month percentile just 27, compressed ~120bps from ~4% at the start of the year; credit markets have not tightened in response to AI capex or breadth weakness

indicators.name_zh: 数据中心 ABS 利差
indicators.threshold_text: 走阔 50bps+ = 红 / 稳定 = 黄 / 收窄 = 绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Widening 50bps+ = red / Stable = yellow / Tightening = green
indicators.note: CloudHQ $1.4B 5Y AAA 4 月以 125bp 定价，较 Switch 3/18 的 185bp 收窄 60bps；CoreWeave 3 月 $8.5B 投资级延伸贷款获 Moody's/Fitch/DBRS A- 等同评级，市场仍报紧利差
indicators.note_en: CloudHQ's $1.4B 5Y AAA priced at 125bp in April, 60bps inside Switch's 185bp print on March 18; CoreWeave's $8.5B IG delayed-draw loan in March cleared Moody's/Fitch/DBRS at A-minus equivalent — market continues to bid tight

indicators.note: CoreWeave / Nebius / Nscale / Crusoe / Lambda 90 日内无下调或违约；CoreWeave 3 月获 IG 评级（A-minus）反而是正向；CoreWeave D/E 4.5x、Nebius 1.1x，杠杆为潜在风险
indicators.note_en: CoreWeave / Nebius / Nscale / Crusoe / Lambda — no downgrades or defaults in 90 days; CoreWeave's March IG rating (A-minus equivalent) is actually a positive; CoreWeave D/E ~4.5x and Nebius 1.1x leverage remain latent risks

indicators.name_zh: AI 行业 Token 月度环比
indicators.threshold_text: 收缩=红 / 减速=黄 / 加速=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Contraction = red / Decel = yellow / Accel = green
indicators.note: OpenRouter 周度 >20T tokens（YoY 4x，2025/4 的 5T → 2026/4 的 20T）；Anthropic 1.3T 周 token、周环比 +28.5%；OpenAI 421B 周 token、周环比 +55.4%；agent 工作流推动 100K-1M tokens / 单请求
indicators.note_en: OpenRouter weekly volume >20T (4x YoY, from 5T in Apr 2025 to 20T in Apr 2026); Anthropic 1.3T weekly tokens, +28.5% WoW; OpenAI 421B weekly tokens, +55.4% WoW; agent workflows push 100K-1M tokens per request

indicators.name_zh: Token 增速 / 收入增速 比值
indicators.note: Anthropic ARR YoY 30x（$1B→$30B），token YoY ~10x，token 增速 / ARR 增速 ≈ 0.3-0.5；Claude Code 5 个月由 $500M→$2.5B；收入仍快于 token，单 token 价值上行
indicators.note_en: Anthropic ARR up 30x YoY ($1B → $30B) vs token volume ~10x — token-growth / ARR-growth ratio ≈ 0.3-0.5; Claude Code went $500M → $2.5B in 5 months; revenue still outpaces token volume, per-token value rising

indicators.name_zh: AI 收入增速的二阶导
indicators.threshold_text: 减速=红 / 平稳=黄 / 加速=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Decel = red / Flat = yellow / Accel = green
indicators.note: Anthropic 4 月 ARR $30B（2 月 $14B → 3 月 $19B → 4 月 $30B，单月增量 $5B → $11B），首次超越 OpenAI 的 $24B（$2B / 月）；二阶导持续为正
indicators.note_en: Anthropic ARR hit $30B in April (Feb $14B → Mar $19B → Apr $30B; monthly increment $5B → $11B), formally surpassing OpenAI's $24B ($2B/month pace); 2nd derivative remains decisively positive

indicators.note: Deloitte 2026 调查：72% 企业至少 1 项 AI workload 已生产，80%+ Fortune 500 跑 AI agent；但仅 25% 完成 ≥40% pilot 转产，转化深度仍不足
indicators.note_en: Deloitte 2026 survey: 72% of enterprises have at least one AI workload in production, 80%+ of Fortune 500 run AI agents; but only 25% have moved ≥40% of pilots to production — conversion depth still lags

indicators.threshold_text: 负增长=红 / 减速=黄 / 加速=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Negative = red / Decel = yellow / Accel = green
indicators.note: MSFT 商业 RPO $392B（+51% YoY，剔除 OpenAI 仍 +26%）、Azure +40% 受供应限制；GCP Q1 收入 +63% 至约 $20B 是绝对亮点；AWS +28%；hyperscaler 积压合计约 $2T
indicators.note_en: MSFT commercial RPO $392B (+51% YoY, +26% ex-OpenAI), Azure +40% supply-constrained; GCP Q1 revenue +63% to ~$20B is the clearest demand signal; AWS +28%; hyperscaler backlog totals ~$2T

indicators.threshold_text: 任何=红 / 调查=黄 / 无=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Any confirmed = red / Investigation = yellow / None = green
indicators.note: Burry 重述 Mag7 2026-28 折旧拉长可能虚增收益 ~$176B（ORCL、META 经济利润可能被高估 20%+），但仍无 SEC 执法或审计师质疑
indicators.note_en: Burry reiterates that lengthened depreciation schedules could overstate Mag7 earnings by ~$176B during 2026-28 (ORCL and META operating income potentially overstated by 20%+), but no SEC enforcement or auditor concerns yet

indicators.threshold_text: 加息=红 / 通胀压力=黄 / 降息=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Hike = red / Inflation pressure = yellow / Cut = green
indicators.note: 4/29 FOMC 第 3 次按兵不动维持 3.5-3.75%，4 名异议（32 年来首次），点阵图全年仅 1 次降息；Powell 5/15 卸任主席（仍留任理事至 2028），Warsh 通过参议院银行委员会，6/16-17 会议由其主持
indicators.note_en: 4/29 FOMC held 3.5-3.75% for a 3rd straight meeting with 4 dissenters (most in 32 years), dot plot still implies just one cut in 2026; Powell steps down as Chair on May 15 (stays as Governor through 2028), Warsh's nomination cleared the Senate Banking Committee and he'll chair the June 16-17 meeting

indicators.name_zh: 资本开支指引市场反应
indicators.threshold_text: 系统性惩罚=红 / 偶发=黄 / 奖励=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Systematic punishment = red / Episodic = yellow / Rewarded = green
indicators.note: Q1 财报后市场分化：META -6%（capex 上调至 $125-145B 触发 $175B 市值蒸发）、MSFT、AMZN 同步下跌；唯独 GOOGL 上涨（GCP 收入 +63% 兑现 capex）；2 家受罚 + 1 家奖励，仍未达系统性
indicators.note_en: Post-Q1 reaction split: META -6% (capex raised to $125-145B, erasing $175B in market cap), MSFT and AMZN also slipped; only GOOGL rallied (GCP revenue +63% validated capex); 2 punished + 1 rewarded — not yet systemic

indicators.threshold_text: 普遍承认过热=红 / 部分=黄 / 无=绿
indicators.threshold_text_en: Widespread acknowledgment = red / Partial = yellow / None = green
indicators.note: Altman："AI 仍在泡沫、有人会损失巨额"；Dimon："比别人更担心、钱会被浪费"；Pichai："无公司能免疫泡沫破裂"；Nadella、Zuckerberg 仍偏建设性；对冲增加但未达普遍承认
indicators.note_en: Altman: 'still in an AI bubble, someone will lose a phenomenal amount'; Dimon: 'far more worried than others, money will probably be lost'; Pichai: 'no company is immune to a bubble bursting'; Nadella and Zuckerberg remain more constructive; hedging is rising but short of universal acknowledgment
